## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi, this is Don Crowder with a video about the pivot. Now, every element in a webinar is vital. But the pivot, it's right up there close to the top in terms of importance. If you do it right, it can do amazing things, but more importantly, if you do it wrong, it can really, really hurt your webinar. Because we're pivoting from content mode into sales mode. To adrupt, we lose people in droves. Now, unfortunately, this causes some webinar presenters to really freak out about this whole pivot thing. But don't worry, I'm going to show you six steps to nail the pivot. It's really not this hard. You simply execute these six steps and you're on your way. But let's talk before I tell you those things, let's talk psychologically what we're doing here. And more importantly, what we want them to feel as a result of the pivot. So remember, the pivot, it's a very small part of your entire webinar. But it's where we point out things to them that they may have not gotten so far. So we want them to feel grateful for the content that they've received already. This is the point of reciprocity, where they want to repay us for the great content that they've already received. We want them to feel a commitment to moving forward so that they will go ahead and buy because they're committed to you and what you're teaching them. We want them to feel inadequate to take the next steps themselves. So they're totally adequate to take them, but they're not adequate to take them themselves. And we want them to have a desire to continue to get more, a fear of missing out in this process, as well as we want them to have this positive momentum to move forward. And even though I recognize that that's a lot, and we have a very short period of time to do this in three to five minutes or four to six minutes in a 90-minute webinar, we can still totally do this by simply by going down a step-by-step process. So the first step is to review what we've covered. So you're listing off the things that you've covered so far in the webinar, and you're showing them the benefits of each of these steps for them. So very quickly, this takes 30 seconds. We've learned this, we've discovered this, we've given this assignment, we've done this. You're going through each one of them and showing them what those are to remind them of the power of the webinar you've presented to them so far. Next, we're working on a commitment to get them to move forward. We're asking them for specific commitments to do X within the next Y time frame,

but you're going to briefly show them of the cool things that they'll get as a result of achieving this objective in their life. And then we go through a series of yes questions. ## [09:06 - 10:24]

What you do is your tone of voice in the pivot just stays exactly the same as it was all the way through the confidence of your sales pitch. And you just continue that on, maybe even putting on a little more enthusiasm. And so, you just roll right through it and people don't even realize they just got pivoted. Next is another way to blow it is to do a how did I do poll? That is like holding up a red flag saying, hey, I'm going to sell you now. People are used to that. They leave in droves when you do that. So, just tell them what you gave them. Don't ask them how you did. Just tell them what you gave them. Next, don't rush through this. Each of these six steps are vital. Don't skip any of them. And finally, don't say anything that implies that the important part of the webinar is now over because the most important part of the webinar is just beginning at this point of the webinar, both for you and more importantly for them. Because from a mindset, what you've given them is only a fraction of the value that they need to get. And the real value they need to get out of your webinar comes by buying the thing that you're going to sell them.
